feed.title: Scholar Alerts — Clean Feed
feed.subtitle: Google Scholar alerts reformatted as individual article entries

entry.title: Empirical evaluation of analytic validity of polygenic scores
entry.content:
Authors: T Lin, J Zeng, SD Gordon, L Wallace, L Ziser, S Shah... - Genome Medicine, 2026

Abstract: Background Polygenic scores (PGSs) are weighted sum scores of trait-associated alleles from up to millions of SNPs. As PGS research pivots to translation into health care settings a key issue for laboratories providing PGS is demonstration of ...

Alert: Nick Martin (author alert)

entry.title: Synch. Live: Collective problem-solving through flocking motion associated with higher connectedness to others
entry.content:
Authors: MI Sas, PAM Mediano, FE Rosas, H Leone, A Sas... - Collective Intelligence, 2026

Abstract: Background: Collective self-organising behaviour is ubiquitous in nature, whereby complex patterns emerge from the local interactions between individuals. Yet in humans, most group behaviour is attributed to explicit central control or pre-planning ...

Tags: Reviewer, too: Complexity

Alert: Fernando E. Rosas (author alert)

entry.title: The genome sequence of Schreibers's Long-fingered Bat, Miniopterus schreibersii (Kuhl, 1817)(Chiroptera: Miniopteridae)
entry.content:
Authors: I Alvarez van Tussenbroek, SC Vernes, H Nicolaou... - Wellcome Open Research, 2026

Abstract: We present a genome assembly from an individual male Miniopterus schreibersii (Schreibers's Long-fingered Bat; Chordata; Mammalia; Chiroptera; Miniopteridae). The assembly contains two haplotypes with total lengths of 1 850.57 megabases and ...

Alert: Sonja Vernes (author alert)

entry.title: The genome sequence of Nathusius' pipistrelle, Pipistrellus nathusii (Keyserling & Blasius, 1839)(Chiroptera: Vespertilionidae)
entry.content:
Authors: M Ruedi, M Mai, EC Teeling, SC Vernes... - Wellcome Open Research, 2026

Abstract: We present a genome assembly from an individual female Pipistrellus nathusii (Nathusius' pipistrelle; Chordata; Mammalia; Chiroptera; Vespertilionidae). The assembly contains two haplotypes with total lengths of 1 961.71 megabases and 1 ...

Alert: Sonja Vernes (author alert)

entry.title: Parasitic infection is associated with reduced latency to socially learn a novel foraging task
entry.content:
Authors: S Abdu, M Chimento, G Alarcón-Nieto, KM Wanelik... - Royal Society Open Science, 2026

Abstract: Social learning, whereby individuals use social information to explore and exploit the environment, is widespread among animals. It enables individuals to have greater efficiency in extracting resources and better adapt to changing environments. At the ...

Alert: Damien Farine (author alert)

entry.title: Ethical Precision in Nanoscale Brain Interfacing
entry.content:
Authors: G Wood - Advanced Science, 2026

Abstract: Recent advances in nanotechnological brain interfacing are transforming precision from a technical achievement into a new form of intimate access to neural and mental life. Nanoscale sensors and stimulators approach synaptic resolution while reducing ...

Tags: brain

Alert: Guilherme M. de O. Wood (author alert)

entry.title: The geology of the Twelve Apostles in the Port Campbell Embayment in southeast Australia
entry.content:
Authors: SJ Gallagher, CW Mallett, R Soman, G Auer, A Herries - Australian Journal of Earth ..., 2026

Abstract: The coastal strata of the Port Campbell region in southeast Australia host several 40– 70 m-high limestone sea stacks called the 'Twelve Apostles' and over 40 km of near continuous sea cliffs. Despite their 'iconic'status, their geology is not well known ...

Alert: Gerald Auer (author alert)
